memes.label: Allhallows
memes.description: The patron saint or divine invocation of the churches on both sides of the Sundering Flood, frequently invoked in oaths, blessings, and prayers by the Dalesmen.

memes.label: Baron of Deepdale
memes.description: A powerful robber-baron and the primary antagonist in the Eastcheaping conflict arc, whose military campaign against Eastcheaping ends in his capture and a humiliating peace treaty.

memes.label: Barons' League
memes.description: A coalition of robber-barons who waged war against the free dales and towns along the Sundering Flood, ultimately defeated by Sir Godrick of Longshaw at the great battle on the first of May.

memes.label: Battle of Longshaw
memes.description: The decisive battle near Longshaw in which Sir Godrick and Osberne the Red Lad, despite being outnumbered three to one, utterly defeated the host of the Barons' League during the great war on the first of May.

memes.label: Battle of the Sundering Flood
memes.description: The overarching armed conflict along the Sundering Flood river, involving Osberne of Wethermel, Sir Godrick of Longshaw, and their allies against hostile forces threatening the Dale.

memes.label: Bight of the Cloven Knoll
memes.description: A bight (bend or bay) in the Sundering Flood near the Cloven Knoll; a notable river-landmark where the flood-plain curves around the knoll, described in Chapter 9.

memes.label: Boardcleaver
memes.description: A legendary sword gifted to Osberne by the mysterious warrior Steelhead, fashioned by the Dwarf-folk in ancient times.

memes.label: Bridget of Wethermel
memes.description: An old kinswoman of seventy winters at Wethermel, Bridget is Osberne's foster-mother and a carline with knowledge of charms and folk-magic, who gave Osberne a protective amulet against evil things.

memes.label: Brookside
memes.description: The chief manor and castle of the Blue Knight (Sir Mark), a pleasant house where Elfhild found refuge under the protection of its lord, located eastward of the Sundering Flood.

memes.label: Castle of Brookside
memes.description: The castle and dwelling of Sir Mark the Blue Knight at Brookside, a defensible and ancient hall where Elfhild finds refuge under the Blue Knight's protection.

memes.label: Chapmen
memes.description: The merchant and trader class in the world of The Sundering Flood, who travelled between towns and markets conducting trade and carrying goods across the land.

memes.description: "There was an old woman, yet not cripple, who dwelt in a stead beside a great river, which none might cross, either by bridge or ford or ferry. But she dwelt not alone, neither was the house her own..."

memes.description: "But when the aforesaid Michaelmas market was, great recourse was there of far-travelled and wise men, and the Carline set herself diligently to learn all she might of suchlike folk. And she had whe..."

memes.label: "Chapter 6: They Fare to the Cloven Mote"
memes.description: "And now the days wore away to winter, and ever thereafter might Osberne do what he would, and go where he would, for as little a lad as he was; but he worked with a good will if he were uncompelled..."

memes.label: "Chapter 60: The Blue Knight Gathers Men and Departs from Brookside"
memes.description: "Wore the days then till the winter came upon them, and though the season was not hard, yet was there but little coming and going about the countryside, that is to say for long journeys; but even so..."

memes.label: "Chapter 61: The Maiden and the Carline Flee to the Grey Sisters"
memes.description: "Now wore away the days of March, and all was peaceable, but no tidings came from Sir Mark, nor forsooth was any looked for so early. The Blue Knight had left but three score of men-at-arms at Brook..."

memes.label: "Chapter 62: They Fall in with Three Chapmen"
memes.description: "Now when the next day was, the Lady of Brookside sent a half score of men-at-arms to the House of the Grey Sisters, and bade them give up to them the Carline and the Maiden, if they had them there...."

memes.description: "The next night after, they were come to but a little way from the end of the mountains, and could see the tilled and peopled lands lying down before them, and this had been no very long day’s journ..."

memes.label: "Chapter 64: The Carline Endeth Her Tale"
memes.description: "When they arose on the morrow they began to think of departure, though they would have kept them in that guesthouse for many days; but both of the twain, and especially the Maiden, deemed that, if ..."

memes.label: "Chapter 65: Osberne and Elfhild Make Themselves Known to Their People"
memes.description: "Therewith the Carline sat down, and there was great cheer and rumour in the hall, and folk wondered what was to come next; but it is not to be said but that they had an inkling of what had befallen..."

memes.label: "Chapter 66: The Lip of the Sundering Flood"
memes.description: "When it was the morning and the sun shone through the house at Wethermel, those two arose and took each other by the hand, and no word they spake together, but went straight to the Sundering Flood,..."

memes.description: "It was some three years after this that weaponed men came down into the Dale. It was told to Osberne, and he took his sword and went to meet them. He came across them as they fared slowly down the ..."

memes.label: "Chapter 68: The Knight of Longshaw Gathereth Force"
memes.description: "The Knight of Longshaw abode at Wethermel in much content, and much it pleased him to look upon the beauty of Elfhild and the fairness of the life that men lived in the Dale. At last he said: “Now ..."

memes.label: "Chapter 7: Of a Newcomer, and His Gift to Osberne"
memes.description: "Now when spring came again, needs must Osberne drive the sheep up to the bents, though he had liefer haunted the riverside, for sore he desired to cross the Flood and find out tidings there. And th..."

memes.label: "Chapter 8: The Goodman Gets a New Hired Man"
memes.description: "Now when he came home to Wethermel he found tidings there, for the goodman had gotten a new hired man, and he showed him to Osberne, who greeted him well: he was a tall man, mild of aspect and spee..."

memes.label: "Chapter 9: The Bight of the Cloven Knoll"
memes.description: "And now it was mid-April, and the goodman dight him to ride to a mote of the neighbours at a stead hight Bullmeads, where the Dalesmen were wont to gather in the spring, that they might ride thence..."

memes.label: The Burgreve
memes.description: The burgreve (burgrave, governor) of the City of the Sundering Flood; an officer responsible for the city's defence and civil order, mentioned in connection with Sir Godrick's election to the office.

memes.label: The City King
memes.description: The King of the City of the Sundering Flood, an increasingly powerless monarch who eventually betrays his city to join the Outland King and is slain by Osberne the Red Lad in the final battle.

memes.label: City of the Sundering Flood
memes.description: The great city at the mouth of the Sundering Flood river, built and thriven because of its excellent haven — a major urban center with walls, gates, two great bridges of barges, the King's palace, the great gildhall, and a complex civic structure of Porte, Great Council, and Burgreve, where the Lesser Crafts rose against tyranny and where Sir Godrick and Osberne fought to establish a new order.

memes.label: Cloven Knoll
memes.description: A distinctive geographical feature on the Sundering Flood river where a knoll had been cast down across the stream, creating a sheltered bight on the eastern side with a cave under a ledge of sheer rock — the trysting place of Osberne and Elfhild, the site of the shot-stour between the Eastdalers and the Black Skinners, and the enduring symbol of love across the uncrossable water.

memes.label: Cloven Mote
memes.description: A traditional gathering of the Dalesmen, held at midwinter and midsummer, serving as both a religious festival and a social assembly for the people of the East and West Dales.

memes.label: The Cloven Mote Tradition
memes.description: The tradition of the Cloven Motes — the communal gatherings of the Dale folk where disputes were settled and common decisions made; the foundational institution of Dale governance and community.

memes.label: Council of the Lesser Crafts
memes.description: A governing assembly of the trades and crafts of the City, presided over by Sir Godrick, which determined civic policy and the conduct of the war.

memes.label: The Dale
memes.description: The dale (valley) region along the Sundering Flood where Wethermel, East Dale, and West Dale lie; the pastoral homeland of Osberne and the Dalesmen, a place of sheep, wolves, Cloven Motes, and proud free folk of "earls' kindred" stock.
aliases: the Dale of the Sundering Flood

memes.label: Dalesmen
memes.description: The free folk dwelling in the East and West Dales along the Sundering Flood, a decentralized community of independent homesteads bound by shared custom, the Cloven Mote, and a fierce commitment to liberty — who gathered to fight against the Barons' League, the Black Skinners, and the King's tyranny.

memes.label: Dame Anna
memes.description: Elfhild's kinswoman and guardian on the west side of the Sundering Flood, who raised her after her parents' death in the pestilence.

memes.label: Deepdale
memes.description: The territory and domain of the Baron of Deepdale, a robber-baron realm lying near Eastcheaping, whose lord waged war against the free town before being captured and forced to peace.

memes.label: Deepdale Conflict
memes.description: A protracted military conflict between the town of Eastcheaping and the Baron of Deepdale, marked by skirmishes, a siege, and the eventual capture of the Baron.

memes.label: Dwarf-wrought Craft
memes.description: The legendary craftsmanship of the Dwarf-folk, makers of enchanted weapons and armor in the world of the Sundering Flood, including Boardcleaver and Steelhead's bow.

memes.label: Eastcheaping Crafts
memes.description: The organized crafts and trades of the market town of Eastcheaping, including the Butchers' guild, which formed the commercial and artisanal backbone of the principal town of the Dales.

memes.label: East Dale
memes.description: The eastern valley of the Dales, home to Osberne of Wethermel and the richer, more populous side of the Sundering Flood valley, containing Wethermel, Longshaw, and the eastern church of Allhallows.

memes.label: Eastdalers
memes.description: The folk dwelling in East Dale, on the eastern side of the Sundering Flood; organised under Osberne's captaincy into a formidable fighting force, they defeat the Baron of Deepdale and march to the City's aid.

memes.label: Elfhild
memes.description: The maiden dwelling on the far side of the Sundering Flood, beloved of Osberne, who receives gifts from the mysterious cave-folk and whose bond with Osberne spans the great river — the co-protagonist of William Morris's The Sundering Flood.

memes.label: The Felons
memes.description: The felons (criminals) who beguile and capture Osberne in Chapter 51, setting up the crisis that leads to his reunion with Elfhild.

memes.label: Geography of The Sundering Flood
memes.description: A survey of the geographic world of The Sundering Flood (1897), tracing the spatial relationships between settlements along the great river and the regions beyond, from Wethermel in the west to the City of the Sundering Flood in the east.

memes.label: Gift-Giving
memes.description: The custom of exchanging gifts across the Sundering Flood, a central motif in the novel through which characters forge and maintain bonds of friendship, love, and loyalty.

memes.label: Goodman of Wethermel
memes.description: The goodman (master) of Wethermel, Osberne's grandsire, a stout man of fifty winters at the tale's beginning, grudging and grumbling in temper but a hard worker on the lonely stead.

memes.label: Goodwife of Wethermel
memes.description: The goodwife of Wethermel, Osberne's grandam, a kindly woman of forty-five winters who treasures the young Osberne and intercedes for him against the goodman's harsh discipline.

memes.label: The Great Council of the City
memes.description: The governing council of the City of the Sundering Flood, composed of the masters of the great and small crafts, which together with the Porte and the Burgreve formed the civic government that eventually replaced the monarchy.

memes.label: Hartshaw Knolls
memes.description: A small stead in the West Dale consisting of two knolls and a shaw of wood, home to Elfhild and her aunts, later raided and laid waste by the Black Skinners.

memes.label: The Hosting of the Barons' League
memes.description: The great military muster of the Barons' League in preparation for their campaign against Sir Godrick of Longshaw, announced in Chapter 59 and gathering forces from across the lands east of the Sundering Flood.

relations.label: King of the City of the Sundering Flood
battle on the first of May

memes.label: Knight of Longshaw
memes.description: The formal chivalric title of Sir Godrick as Lord of Longshaw, by which he is known throughout the latter half of the novel as the leader of the uprising against the Barons' League.

memes.label: Knight of the Fish
memes.description: Sir Raynold Fisher, the lord of the Castle of the Fish, who maintained the century-old custom of jousting with all armed parties passing his lands, and was overthrown by Sir Godrick and Osberne in a friendly contest.

memes.label: Longryggs
memes.description: A farmstead in the West Dale, the first substantial settlement south of Hartshaw Knolls, which was raided by the Black Skinners during their invasion and became the site of a pitched battle between the Westdalers and the raiders.

memes.label: Lord of Longshaw
memes.description: The lord of Longshaw, whom Sir Godrick serves; a noble figure of the feudal order, mentioned in connection with the Barons' League politics and Sir Godrick's allegiance.

memes.label: Midsummer Feast
memes.description: The great summer festival held at the time of the summer solstice, combining religious observance, the Midsummer Feast of the Cloven Mote, seasonal markets, and folk customs in the world of The Sundering Flood.

memes.label: Osberne
memes.description: The protagonist of The Sundering Flood, a shepherd boy of Wethermel who rises to become a great warrior known as the Red Lad, and whose love for Elfhild spans the great river that divides them.

memes.label: The Outland King
memes.description: A foreign monarch from oversea who threatened the City of the Sundering Flood with invasion, allied briefly with the City King, but was ultimately defeated by Sir Godrick's forces and driven back across the sea.

memes.label: Parley from the Walls
memes.description: The parley between the Dalesmen and the forces of the City and the Baron, held from the walls of Eastcheaping, leading to terms of peace in the Deepdale conflict.

memes.label: The Pipe and Necklace
memes.description: Two magical gifts given to Elfhild by the Dwarfs of the cave — a golden pipe whose music had the power to calm beasts and ward off harm, and a necklace of gold and priceless gems — which she received by wishing for them in the Dwarf-cave above the Sundering Flood.

memes.label: Porte of Eastcheaping
memes.description: The governing body of the town of Eastcheaping, combining civic administration with military command, responsible for the town's defense and diplomatic affairs.

memes.label: Red Lad
memes.description: The war-name and epithet by which Osberne of Wethermel became known among the Dalesmen and their allies during the wars against the Barons' League.

memes.label: Sir Alwyn
memes.description: An aged but stout knight, lord of Warding Knowe and dear friend of Sir Mark the Blue Knight, known for his wisdom, hospitality, and prophetic foresight of his own death in battle.

memes.label: Sir Degore
memes.description: A hoar and wise knight serving as a commander under the Baron of Deepdale, who confronted the Eastcheaping forces after the Baron's capture.

memes.label: Skirmish in the Marshes
memes.description: An early skirmish between Osberne's Dalesmen and the forces of the Baron of Deepdale in the marshy lands near Deepdale, recounted in Chapter 24.

memes.label: Slaying of Hardcastle
memes.description: The confrontation at Wethermel in which Osberne, wielding the dwarf-wrought sword Boardcleaver, slew the would-be usurper Hardcastle who sought to seize the stead.

memes.label: The Small Crafts
memes.description: The lesser craft-guilds of the City of the Sundering Flood, representing the common working folk against the Porte and the Great Crafts; they become a key political force aligned with Sir Godrick and the Red Lad.

memes.label: The Staves
memes.description: The quarterstaves — long wooden pole-weapons — that Osberne teaches the Dalesmen to use as their distinctive weapon of war, transforming them from pastoral folk into a formidable fighting force.

memes.label: Steelhead
memes.description: A mysterious warrior and mentor to Osberne, who bestows the dwarf-wrought sword Boardcleaver and other magical gifts upon the young shepherd, appearing at crucial moments throughout the tale.

memes.label: Sundering Flood
memes.description: The mighty river that gives the novel its name, running north to south from mountain sources to the sea, dividing the East Dale from the West and shaping every aspect of life in the Dale — serving as both an impassable barrier and, paradoxically, the bond that unites the two lovers Osberne and Elfhild.

memes.label: The Chapman
memes.description: A travelling merchant (chapman) who buys Elfhild from the Red Skinners and later sells her to the Blue Knight; his greed sets in motion the chain of events that leads Elfhild eastward toward Brookside and eventual reunion with Osberne.
aliases: the chapman who bought the Maiden

memes.label: The Flood as Symbol
memes.description: The Sundering Flood as the central symbolic device of the novel — a barrier of separation that defines the love story, a test of devotion, and a geographical embodiment of the distance between two worlds.

memes.label: The Maiden
memes.description: The archetypal figure of the Maiden in The Sundering Flood, embodied primarily by Elfhild but recurring throughout the narrative as a symbol of lost love, quest-motivation, and the object of the hero's devotion in William Morris's medieval romance.

memes.label: The Marshes
memes.description: The marshy lands near Deepdale where Osberne's Dalesmen skirmish with the Baron's forces; difficult terrain that favours the mobile stave-wielding Dalesmen over heavy feudal cavalry.

memes.label: The North Gate
memes.description: The northern gate of the City of the Sundering Flood; one of the key entry points to the city, mentioned in the context of military movements and the arrival of forces during the Barons' League conflict.

memes.label: The Wood
memes.description: The woodland regions bordering the settled lands along the Sundering Flood; a liminal wild space associated with Dwarf-kin, outlaws, and adventure, standing in contrast to the pastoral Dale and the urban City.

memes.label: Themes of The Sundering Flood
memes.description: A survey of the major literary themes in The Sundering Flood (1897) by William Morris — isolation, community, growth, love across separation, and the tension between rural and urban life — as expressed through the novel's characters, settings, and narrative structure.

memes.label: Thorp-Dwellers
memes.description: The village-dwellers (thorp-folk) of the settled lands whom Osberne and Sir Godrick rescue from the Black Skinners, demonstrating the knights' role as protectors of the common people.

memes.label: Timeline of The Sundering Flood
memes.description: A chronological overview of the narrative arc of The Sundering Flood (1897), tracing Osberne's journey from boyhood at Wethermel through the Eastcheaping war, the quest for Elfhild, and the final reunion, across 68 chapters.

memes.label: The Trysting-Place
memes.description: The trysting-place along the Sundering Flood where Osberne and Elfhild meet across the river, first by speech and later in person after Osberne's return, serving as the emotional anchor of their bond throughout the tale.

memes.label: War of the Dales
memes.description: The series of military campaigns in which the Dalesmen, led by Osberne the Red Lad and the Knight of Longshaw, fought against the Barons' League and their allies.

memes.label: Warding Knowe
memes.description: A fair white castle on a knoll surrounded by a little river, the stronghold of Sir Alwyn, a friend of the Blue Knight, and a bastion against the strong-thieves of the forest.

memes.label: Waywearer
memes.description: The travelling alias used by Steelhead when he first visits Wethermel and bestows magical gifts upon Osberne's household.

memes.label: West Dale
memes.description: The western valley of the Dales, home to Elfhild and the western church of Allhallows at the Cloven Mote, separated from the East Dale by the Sundering Flood.

memes.label: Westdalers
memes.description: The folk dwelling in West Dale, on the western side of the Sundering Flood, including Elfhild and her kinswoman's household; they suffer from raiders and are less organised than the Eastdalers under Osberne's leadership.

memes.label: Wethermel
memes.description: The remote homestead on the east side of the Sundering Flood where Osberne was born and raised — a lonely stead whose isolation shaped the boy who would become the Red Lad.

memes.label: Winter of Fear
memes.description: A prophesied and feared winter of such extreme cold that the Sundering Flood would freeze over with ice, allowing passage between the eastern and western sides — an event invoked as both a fear and a forbidden hope.

memes.label: Wood Masterless
memes.description: A great forest on the eastern side of the Sundering Flood, lying between Wethermel and Longshaw, inhabited by wild beasts and serving as a border region between the settled dales.

memes.label: Woodneb
memes.description: A stead in the East Dale near the Cloven Mote, used as an assembly point for Dalesmen, and later a fortified outpost under Sir Godrick's captain Edward the Brown at the edge of the Wood Masterless.

memes.label: Wulfgrimsson
memes.description: One of Osberne's trusted men among the Eastdalers; mentioned as a companion and follower during the Dalesmen's campaigns.

memes.label: Yule
memes.description: The great winter festival celebrated throughout the Dales and the lands along the Sundering Flood, marked by feasting, gift-giving, and the gathering of war-farers at Longshaw.
